segments.text: Job continued his discourse:
How I long for the months gone by, for the days when God watched over me,
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: when his lamp shone on my head and by his light I walked through darkness!
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Oh, for the days when I was in my prime, when God's intimate friendship blessed my house,
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: when the Almighty was still with me and my children were around me,
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: when my path was drenched with cream and the rock poured out for me streams of olive oil.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: When I went to the gate of the city and took my seat in the public square,
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: the young men saw me and stepped aside and the old men rose to their feet;
the chief men refrained from speaking and covered their mouths with their hands;
the voices of the nobles were hushed, and their tongues stuck to the roof of their mouths.
Whoever heard me spoke well of me, and those who saw me commended me,
because I rescued the poor who cried for help, and the fatherless who had none to assist them.
The one who was dying blessed me; I made the widow's heart sing.
I put on righteousness as my clothing; justice was my robe and my turban.
I was eyes to the blind and feet to the lame.
I was a father to the needy; I took up the case of the stranger.
I broke the fangs of the wicked and snatched the victims from their teeth.
I thought, 'I will die in my own house, my days as numerous as the grains of sand.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: My roots will reach to the water, and the dew will lie all night on my branches.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: My glory will not fade; the bow will be ever new in my hand.'
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: People listened to me expectantly, waiting in silence for my counsel.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: After I had spoken, they spoke no more; my words fell gently on their ears.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: They waited for me as for showers and drank in my words as the spring rain.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: When I smiled at them, they scarcely believed it; the light of my face was precious to them.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: I chose the way for them and sat as their chief; I dwelt as a king among his troops; I was like one who comforts mourners.
segments.speaker: Job
